content.full_text:
February 18, 1977

Honorable Bill Lockyer  
Member of the Assembly  
State Capitol  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Assemblyman Lockyer:

This letter will confirm advice given to your administrative aide, Paul Meyer, concerning whether or not a billboard donated to you by Foster and Kleiser to announce the time and location of meetings with constituents is a reportable campaign contribution.

2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215, which clarifies the definition of what constitutes a contribution, states:

"Contribution" includes any monetary or non-monetary payment for which full and adequate consideration is not made to the donor that is received by or made at the behest of:

(1) A candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding circumstances that the payment was received or made at his behest for personal purposes unrelated to his candidacy or status as an officeholder....

The billboard is clearly being provided to you because of your status as an officeholder and based on the above regulation is a contribution to your campaign. The fair market value of the billboard must, therefore, be reported on your campaign statement as a non-monetary contribution from Foster and Kleiser.

Please contact me if I may be of additional assistance.

Sincerely,

REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

February 2, 1977

John Giansello  
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Post Office Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Giansello:

In the near future I and other public officials will hold a pair of "town meetings" at different locations in my home district. The public will be invited to come and discuss current public policy issues. The purpose of the meetings will simply be to encourage communication between the citizenry and their elected representatives.

As a public service, Foster and Kleiser, a billboard firm, has offered to provide free billboard space to announce the time and location of these meetings. My question to you is whether or not their offer would be considered a campaign donation which would therefore be reportable to the FPPC.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Best wishes,

BILL LOCKYER  
Assemblyman
